s110.title: Enforcement Notices
s110.auditorFocus: If the Commissioner has previously issued an enforcement notice to the organisation, JPDP inspectors will specifically verify that it has been fully complied with. Evidence of compliance with every requirement specified in the notice must be produced. Partial or late compliance is treated as a continuing offence under s113.

evidenceItems.name: Enforcement notice compliance evidence package
evidenceItems.description: A structured file containing the enforcement notice, the organisation's compliance plan, and evidence of completion of every remedial step specified in the notice, with dates
whatGoodLooksLike: Compliance plan was prepared and submitted to the Commissioner within the timeline specified in the notice
Each requirement in the notice has corresponding evidence of compliance (screenshots, system configuration records, policy documents, training records as applicable)
Compliance was confirmed to the Commissioner in writing before or by the deadline specified in the notice
All evidence is timestamped, demonstrating that remedial steps were taken before the deadline
Legal counsel review of the compliance evidence package is documented before submission to the Commissioner
Board or senior management sign-off on the compliance plan is documented
commonGaps: Organisation received an enforcement notice but cannot produce evidence of any remedial steps taken
Remedial steps were taken after the deadline without seeking a Commissioner extension
Only some requirements from the notice were addressed — others were overlooked or deprioritised
Evidence of compliance exists but was never formally submitted to the Commissioner
Compliance was claimed but the remedial steps do not actually address the violation identified in the notice
suggestedSources: Copy of the enforcement notice received
Compliance plan prepared in response to the notice
Evidence package for each remedial requirement (tagged to the specific notice requirement)
Written confirmation of compliance submitted to the Commissioner
Commissioner acknowledgement of compliance
Board or senior management meeting minutes authorising the compliance response

evidenceItems.name: Enforcement notice remediation project records
evidenceItems.description: Project management records demonstrating that the remediation required by an enforcement notice was managed as a priority project with dedicated resources and regular progress reporting
whatGoodLooksLike: Project plan with specific tasks, owners, and target dates aligned to the enforcement notice deadline
Weekly progress reports showing status of each remediation task against the deadline
Board or senior management weekly briefings on remediation progress
Named senior manager accountable for delivery of the remediation project
Resource allocation records showing dedicated staff or budget assigned to the project
Final project completion sign-off confirming all requirements are met before submission to the Commissioner
commonGaps: No project management structure was applied — remediation was ad hoc with no tracking
Progress was not reported to senior management, resulting in insufficient priority given to the work
Deadline was missed because resources were not dedicated and the work competed with normal business priorities
No named accountable person — responsibility was diffused across multiple teams with no single owner
suggestedSources: Project plan and timeline for enforcement notice remediation
Weekly progress reports
Senior management briefing notes
Resource allocation approvals (budget/staff)$

auditTips: Check the JPDP investigation history for the organisation — have any enforcement notices been issued? If so, verify full compliance evidence
Ask the DPO: 'Have you received any enforcement notices from the Commissioner?' and cross-reference the answer
If an enforcement notice was received and compliance is claimed, test a sample of the remedial steps by independently verifying technical changes were implemented
Check whether the compliance confirmation was submitted to the Commissioner before the deadline

s113.title: Failure to Comply with Enforcement Notice
s113.auditorFocus: Section 113 creates a separate and additional criminal liability for failure to comply with an enforcement notice. JPDP inspectors will specifically assess whether enforcement notice compliance is complete, on time, and verifiable. Even good faith partial compliance requires proactive communication with the Commissioner.

evidenceItems.name: Proactive compliance communication to Commissioner
evidenceItems.description: Evidence that where full compliance by the enforcement notice deadline was not achievable, the organisation proactively communicated this to the Commissioner with a revised timetable and evidence of good faith efforts
whatGoodLooksLike: Communication to the Commissioner was sent before the deadline (not after a breach was discovered)
Communication includes specific reasons why the deadline cannot be met and a revised realistic timetable
Communication is accompanied by evidence of remediation steps already completed
Communication is in writing, reviewed by Legal counsel, and approved by senior management before despatch
Commissioner's response acknowledging the revised timetable is on file
commonGaps: The deadline passed without any communication to the Commissioner — the organisation hoped the failure would go unnoticed
Communication was sent after the Commissioner enquired about compliance status (reactive rather than proactive)
Communication was sent but did not include a revised timetable or evidence of partial completion
Communication was sent by email without legal review and contained statements prejudicial to the organisation's position
suggestedSources: Written communication to the Commissioner regarding deadline extension
Commissioner's acknowledgement or response to extension request
Evidence of partial compliance steps completed by the original deadline
Legal counsel review record for the communication

auditTips: If an enforcement notice deadline was missed, verify whether the Commissioner was notified proactively before the deadline or after
Where partial compliance is claimed, verify through independent technical testing that the claimed steps were actually implemented
Ask for the Commissioner's written response to any extension request — this shows the Commissioner's position on the missed deadline

s114.title: Powers of Enforcement Officers
s114.auditorFocus: JPDP inspectors will assess whether the organisation is capable of responding professionally and cooperatively to an enforcement visit, without obstruction and without inadvertent prejudice to its legal position. Staff training and a documented enforcement visit protocol are the key readiness indicators.

evidenceItems.name: Enforcement visit protocol and staff training records
evidenceItems.description: A documented procedure for managing enforcement officer visits, and evidence that staff have been trained on their obligations when JPDP enforcement officers attend the premises
whatGoodLooksLike: Enforcement visit protocol specifies: who to notify immediately (DPO, Legal, CEO), where enforcement officers should be received, how document production should be managed, and what records should be kept of the visit
Protocol instructs staff not to obstruct, and to refer all questions to the DPO
Protocol specifies that legal counsel must be notified within 30 minutes of enforcement officer attendance
Training records show all reception, security, and senior staff have received training on the protocol
Protocol includes a document production log template to record what is produced to enforcement officers
commonGaps: No enforcement visit protocol exists — staff have no guidance on how to respond to an unannounced enforcement visit
Protocol exists but was never communicated to staff below senior management level
Training on enforcement visit procedures has never been conducted
No mechanism exists to document what documents were produced to enforcement officers, leaving the organisation with no record of the production
suggestedSources: Enforcement visit protocol document
Staff training records covering enforcement visit procedures
Document production log template used during enforcement visits
Records from any actual past enforcement visits

evidenceItems.name: Controlled document production evidence
evidenceItems.description: Evidence that the organisation produces documents to enforcement officers in a controlled and documented manner, maintaining a record of everything produced
whatGoodLooksLike: Document production log records each document produced during the enforcement visit: document title, date, recipient officer name, and producing officer name
Documents are produced in copies rather than originals where possible, so the organisation retains a record of what was produced
Electronic data is provided in controlled exports rather than by granting live system access to enforcement officers
Legal counsel is present (in person or by phone) during document production to advise on privilege or other grounds for withholding specific documents
Post-visit review is conducted by Legal counsel to confirm that all produced documents are accounted for and that no privileged material was inadvertently produced
commonGaps: No document production log was maintained during an actual enforcement visit
Original documents were handed over without retaining copies
Enforcement officers were given unmonitored direct access to systems rather than receiving controlled exports
Legal counsel was not present or engaged during the document production process
suggestedSources: Document production logs from any past enforcement visits
Post-visit review records
Legal counsel engagement records during enforcement visits

auditTips: Ask for the enforcement visit protocol and verify it has been tested or briefed to front-desk and security staff who would first encounter enforcement officers
Ask whether any enforcement visits have occurred in the past 5 years — if so, request the document production log
Ask the DPO to walk through what would happen if enforcement officers arrived unannounced at the reception desk right now — the coherence of the response indicates preparedness
